title: Mandrel Self-Benchmark Scorecard
description: One persisted, schema-validated scorecard for a single (scenario × arm × run) of the Mandrel Self-Benchmark Harness (Epic #4211). Each scorecard captures the five measured dimensions for one headless `claude -p` session, stamped with the pinned model id, the framework version under test, and the execution environment so that cross-version / cross-model comparisons only ever compare like to like. Internal tooling only — never shipped in the distributed `.agents/` bundle. Per-run scorecards aggregate into distributions across N≈8–10 runs (see bench/metrics/variance.js for the noise-band method). Authored by Story #4215; consumed by the scoring, report, and persistence slices.

schemaVersion.description: Scorecard-record schema version. Bumped on any breaking change to this record shape (hard cutover — see .agents/rules/git-conventions.md § Contract Cutovers).

runId.description: Unique identifier for this single (scenario × arm × run). Stable across re-reads of the same persisted scorecard.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp at which the run completed and the scorecard was emitted.

model.description: The pinned model under measurement. The harness measures the framework's value-add at a fixed model — the exact id is recorded so a cross-version comparison only compares like model to like.

id.description: Exact model id as reported by the `claude -p` envelope (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8[1m]').

frameworkVersion.description: The Mandrel framework version under test — the `version` of the pinned `mandrel` dependency (node_modules/mandrel/package.json), read by bench/run.js#readFrameworkVersion — for the Mandrel arm. For the control arm this records the framework version the comparison is anchored to, even though the control clone carries no .agents/ scaffolding. Distinct from `benchmarkVersion` (this benchmark repo's own version).

benchmarkVersion.description: The benchmark harness version this record was produced under — THIS repo's own `version` (the mandrel-bench package.json version, read by bench/run.js#readBenchmarkVersion), NOT the pinned `mandrel` dependency version that `frameworkVersion` records. The benchmark is itself a variable: scoring formulas, scenario specs, and oracles all live in this repo, so a benchmark change can move numbers with no framework or model change at all. Per D-014 (docs/target-architecture.md § 3.1) it JOINS `model` + `frameworkVersion` + `env` to form the triple cohort key: noise-bands, deltas, and top-up counting only ever pool records that match on the full stamp, and a corpus mixing benchmark versions in one (model, frameworkVersion) cell is labelled non-inferential rather than silently pooled.

env.description: Execution environment stamp. Comparisons across environments are advisory only; a band is computed per (model, frameworkVersion, env) cohort.

node.description: Node.js version the harness ran under (e.g. 'v24.16.0').

scenario.description: Scenario this run exercised — the Epic #66 3-rung corpus, by rising difficulty: 'hello-world' (the overhead floor + end-to-end smoke, instrumentation only — never a value-delta rung), 'story-scope' (routing: story — persisted-auth API with per-user notes; carries a `trap` block for plaintext-password + token-generation), and 'epic-scope' (routing: epic — multi-user project/task management API; carries a `trap` block for plaintext-password, idor, missing-input-validation, and hardcoded-secret). The prior, smaller-difficulty-ladder corpus was retired in full when this 3-rung matrix landed; its scenario ids no longer appear in persisted scorecards. 'brownfield-longitudinal' (issue #124, B4) is the touch-CHAIN rung over the frozen Ledgerline seed: its record carries a `chain` block instead of `touch2`, and its record-level `dimensions` is a per-touch mean (warning: chain-aggregate-dimensions).

arm.description: Which arm produced this run. 'mandrel' clones carry .agents/ and run the /plan→/deliver pipeline; 'control' clones have no scaffolding and receive the bare task prompt. Ticket #123 adds two OPT-IN variant arms (additional cells within the same cohort — D-014 identity unchanged): 'control-claudemd' (arm 3) is the identical control path plus one static generic CLAUDE.md seeded into the workspace, and 'mandrel-story-routed' (arm 4) is the full mandrel pipeline with the plan-phase prompt forcing single-standalone-Story routing (its expected routing is 'story' by construction, so the routing-mismatch exclusion compares against that override — see bench/driver/arms.js). All arms are costed by the same `claude -p` envelope (apples-to-apples by construction).

routingVerdict.description: Which delivery route Mandrel actually took for this mandrel-arm cell: 'epic' (an Epic lifecycle ledger was found), 'story' (the standalone single-Story path — telemetry recovered from GitHub per the standalone adapter), or null (control arm, or routing could not be determined). Lets the report segment standalone vs Epic cells and explain a null overhead-ratio.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 (target-architecture §8, 'loud nulls'): record-level operator-visible warning markers — dimension-level absent-signal/judge markers (see securityDimension.warnings / maintainabilityDimension.warnings) plus 'standalone-telemetry-absent' when a mandrel-arm run produced neither an Epic lifecycle ledger nor recovered standalone Story telemetry (planning fidelity, autonomy, and the overhead split are all genuinely unmeasured for that record, not silently defaulted).

routingMismatch.description: Epic #66, Story #76: true when a mandrel-arm run's OBSERVED `routingVerdict` diverges from the scenario contract's DECLARED `routing` (scenario.json). Such a record measured a different pipeline than the scenario promises, so bench/report/render.js's `groupCells` excludes it from the cell's noise-band pool and counts it toward the cell's per-cell mismatch rate (>25% is itself a scope-triage calibration finding). false for the control arm, for an undetermined observed verdict, and for every non-diverging record.

landed.description: Ticket #121, item 1: whether the mandrel delivery LANDED on the sandbox default branch (its change-request PR merged), recorded SEPARATELY from whether the delivered tree was scoreable. When a PR does not auto-merge, the harness now scores the PR-HEAD delivery-branch tree (quality/traps/maintainability/security) and sets `landed: false` — a reliability failure that feeds the autonomy dimension — instead of discarding the run as a null on the post-reset baseline tree. `true` = merged onto the default branch. Present on mandrel-arm records only; ABSENT for the control arm (which commits directly, so landing is not a concept) and when landing could not be determined (undetermined ⇒ not scored as a failure).

dimensions.description: The seven measured dimensions for this run. Value side: quality, planningFidelity, autonomy, maintainability, security. Cost side: efficiency, overheadRatio. Each is a single per-run value; the distribution/noise-band is computed across N runs by the scoring slice, never stored here. Formulas are defined in bench/metrics/README.md.

phases.description: Per-phase session envelopes for the mandrel arm's ordered two-session run (D-019, Epic #86 Story #94, docs/target-architecture.md §8). The mandrel arm runs `/plan` (session 1) then `/deliver` (session 2) as separate headless `claude -p` sessions, each with its own cost/tokens/wall-clock, so a finding can be attributed to the planning half vs the delivery half. The per-phase `costUsd` and `tokens` SUM to this record's `dimensions.efficiency.costUsd` and `dimensions.efficiency.totalTokens` by construction (the run envelope is the sum of the phase envelopes). Present on mandrel-arm records only; ABSENT for the control arm (a single session) — a control record is valid without it.

rawRefs.description: Pointers to the on-disk raw artifacts this scorecard was derived from, for traceability and re-scoring. Paths are workspace-relative; the underlying ephemeral workspace is torn down after the run, so these are provenance breadcrumbs, not live handles.

lifecycleNdjson.description: Path to the run's temp/run-<id>/lifecycle.ndjson (timings/autonomy source).

costEnvelope.description: Path to the captured `claude -p` terminal usage/cost envelope (the stream's `type:"result"` event).

transcripts.description: Paths to the gzipped per-phase `claude -p --output-format stream-json` event streams (`<phase>-transcript.ndjson.gz`), one per session the cell ran (control: `session`; mandrel: `plan` + `deliver`). They carry the per-TURN record that the terminal envelope aggregates away, which is what makes turn-level cost attribution possible after the sandbox is gone (Story #154). Capture is best-effort: the key is ABSENT when no transcript could be written.

planMetricsJson.description: Path to the captured plan-invocation ledger (temp/run-<id>/plan-metrics.json or temp/standalone/plan-metrics.json), which records each plan CLI invocation the cell made. Capture is best-effort: the key is ABSENT when the workspace produced no ledger or the copy failed (Story #155).

acceptanceEvalVerdict.description: Path to the acceptance-eval LLM-judge verdict JSON, validated against .agents/schemas/acceptance-eval-verdict.schema.json.

phaseEnvelope.description: One phase-scoped `claude -p` session envelope for the mandrel arm's ordered two-session run (D-019). `costUsd`/`tokens` across the phases sum to the record's efficiency totals.

phase.description: Which pipeline phase this session drove: 'plan' (session 1, `/plan`) or 'deliver' (session 2, `/deliver`).

costUsd.description: USD cost of this phase's session from its `claude -p` envelope, or null when the envelope reported none.

tokens.description: Total tokens (input + output + cache) for this phase's session.

wallClockMs.description: This phase session's own `claude -p` duration in milliseconds. Distinct from the record's ledger-derived `efficiency.wallClockMs`; the per-phase wall-clocks are the envelope session durations.

planQualityBlock.description: Intrinsic PLAN-QUALITY axis (Epic #86, Story #95; D-019, docs/target-architecture.md §8). Scores the pre-delivery plan the mandrel arm's `/plan` session produced — the frozen snapshot under `.raw/<run-stamp>/plan/` — against the scenario's frozen spec, so a bad outcome can be attributed to the plan phase vs the deliver phase. A MANDREL-ONLY axis: the control arm authors no plan, so its plan-quality is null (or the block is absent), and — like planning-fidelity and autonomy — it is DELIBERATELY excluded from the Mandrel-vs-control differential (`SCALAR_DIMENSIONS` in bench/score/differential.js). It lives at the scorecard's top level (a sibling of `dimensions`, like `trap`), never under `dimensions`. Composite = 0.7 deterministic spine (mean of coverage, decompositionSanity, constraintSurfacing) + 0.3 LLM-judge cross-check, with the judge weight folding into the spine when the judge is null.

score.description: Composite plan quality in [0,1]. Deterministic spine weighted 0.7; LLM-judge cross-check weighted 0.3 (judge weight folds into the spine when the cross-check is null).

coverage.description: Fraction of the scenario's FROZEN acceptance criteria traceable to a Story AC in the plan snapshot.

decompositionSanity.description: Story count/sizing vs the scenario's machine-readable `storyCountContract` (epic-scope 4-6 Stories; the story-routed rungs a single standalone Story). Null when no contract was supplied (the sub-score folds out of the spine mean).

constraintSurfacing.description: Fraction of the security-baseline obligations the scenario's trap classes probe that are surfaced in the plan artifacts. 1 for a scenario with no trap obligations (e.g. hello-world).

judgeScore.description: LLM-judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run (the 0.3 judge weight then folds into the spine).

plannedStoryCount.description: Number of Stories the plan snapshot recorded (the decomposition-sanity input).

warnings.description: Loud-null markers: 'plan-quality-decomposition-contract-absent' when the scenario supplied no storyCountContract, 'plan-quality-judge-absent' when the judge cross-check did not run.

attribution.description: The D-019 §3.4 attribution decision table computed for this run — plan quality × delivered outcome × plan-adherence → one of the four classes. Crossing all three inputs is the Goodhart backstop: a plan that games the spine cannot classify as working-as-intended without a matching outcome.

classification.description: The attribution verdict, or null when plan quality or outcome was unmeasured (the table cannot attribute without both).

planGood.description: True when planQuality.score ≥ the plan threshold (default 0.7).

outcomeGood.description: True when the delivered outcome (frozen quality score) ≥ the outcome threshold (default 0.7).

adhered.description: True when plan-adherence (planningFidelity.score) ≥ the adherence threshold (default 0.7); null when unmeasured.

trapBlock.description: Multi-class differential trap signal (Epic #66, Story #74 — replaces the single-class 'trapSignal' shape). One entry per planted defect class the scenario declares under bench/scenarios/<id>/traps/<class>.js, aggregated by bench/scenarios/trap-runner.js. Present only when the scenario declares at least one trap class; absent for every other scenario. Deliberately NOT folded into the seven composite dimensions — this is a separate differential axis the frozen Quality suite cannot see (both arms can pass the behavioural suite identically).

classes.description: One verdict per declared trap class this run was scored against.

cleanRate.description: Mean of the per-class `score` values in `classes`. Higher is better: 1 = every declared trap class is clean, 0 = every class shows the planted defect.

trapClassEntry.description: One trap class's verdict, produced by a per-class oracle module (bench/scenarios/<id>/traps/<class>.js) exporting evaluate(deliveredTreePath).

class.description: The planted defect class this oracle scores (e.g. 'plaintext-password', 'idor', 'token-generation', 'missing-input-validation', 'hardcoded-secret').

score.description: 1 = clean (planted defect absent), 0 = defect present. Higher is better, matching the other value dimensions.

defectPresent.description: True ⇒ the delivered code took the planted defect-class shortcut. False ⇒ clean.

evidence.description: Human-readable justification lines for the verdict. Never echoes a captured password or secret value — derived signals only.

touch2Block.description: Second-touch continuity block (Epic #86, Story #96). After touch 1 is scored, the driver runs the scenario's FROZEN change request as a fresh session against the delivered tree with arm-appropriate inheritance (mandrel keeps its full pipeline output; the control workspace is reduced to delivered code only), then scores the second touch with the full dimension set plus its own frozen behavioural suite and the phase-scoped `traps-touch2/` regression oracles. Present only when the scenario declares a `changeRequest` AND the second touch was scored; ABSENT for touch-1-only scenarios (e.g. hello-world). Lives at the scorecard's top level (a sibling of `trap`/`phases`), never folded into the seven composite dimensions. The report/dashboard render the CONTINUITY DELTA (mandrel `outcome`/`cost` − control `outcome`/`cost`) from this block.

changeRequestId.description: Stable id of the frozen change request this second touch exercised (scenario.json `changeRequest.id`, e.g. 'password-change', 'project-sharing').

inheritance.description: Arm-appropriate inheritance the second touch ran under: 'full-pipeline' (mandrel — the delivered tree with its `.agents` overlay + tickets/plan state intact) or 'delivered-code-only' (control — a fresh copy reduced to the shipped code, with framework/session artifacts stripped).

materialized.description: Whether the change-request code actually LANDED before scoring. The mandrel arm pulls the merged change via `git reset --hard origin/main`; when its PR does not auto-merge, `origin/main` is unchanged and the reset restores the touch-1 tree. `false` means the change never materialized — `outcome` is `null` (unmeasured, NOT a fabricated 0 from a stale tree) and this cell is excluded from the continuity outcome delta; the failed unattended landing is itself an autonomy signal. Absent/`true` means the delivered change was scored normally (the control arm commits directly, so it always materializes).

outcome.description: Continuity OUTCOME scalar in [0,1] — the second touch's composite quality (its `dimensions.quality.score`). The mandrel-minus-control difference of this value is the continuity outcome delta.

cost.description: Continuity COST scalar in USD — the second touch's session cost (its `dimensions.efficiency.costUsd`), or null when the envelope reported none. The mandrel-minus-control difference of this value is the continuity cost delta.

frozenSuitePassed.description: Count of passing frozen touch-2-suite assertions.

totalTokens.description: Total tokens (input + output + cache) for the second-touch session.

wallClockMs.description: The second-touch session's wall-clock duration in milliseconds.

dimensions.description: The FULL dimension set scored for the second touch — the same seven-dimension shape as touch 1's `dimensions`, computed over the second-touch delivered tree.

chainBlock.description: Touch-chain block (issue #124, PR-C; brownfield-longitudinal design §3/§5). For a scenario declaring `touches[]`, the seed overlay IS the baseline and the cell runs N declared touches as a CHAIN: each touch's session runs against the CURRENT chain baseline, is scored (evolved frozen suite + convention oracles + full dimensions), and either ADVANCES the baseline (delivered && baseSuitePassRate ≥ advanceThreshold && appBoots ⇒ the scored tree is force-pushed as the new baseline) or is skipped forward (the next touch seeds from the last-good tree). ONE scorecard per cell (the D-014/checkpoint invariant): the per-touch results live in `touches[]` here, the record-level `dimensions` is the MEAN over materialized touches (stamped with the `chain-aggregate-dimensions` warning), and this block is a sibling of `trap`/`touch2`/`phases` — never folded into the seven composite dimensions. Mutually exclusive with `touch2` (a chain scenario declares no `changeRequest`).

advanceThreshold.description: The base-suite pass-rate gate a touch must clear to advance the chain baseline (`scenario.chainAdvanceThreshold`, default 0.90).

landedCount.description: Touches whose change LANDED: merged onto the sandbox default branch (landed:true), plus advanced touches on arms where landing is not a concept (the control arm commits directly). An unlanded-but-advanced mandrel touch is NOT counted — its spend still appears in the numerator of costPerLandedChange (the autonomy penalty in dollars).

costPerLandedChange.description: Σ every touch's session cost (landed or not) ÷ landedCount, in USD; null when no touch landed.

touches.description: Per-touch chain entries, in chain order (touchIndex 1..N).

chainTouchEntry.description: One touch of a chain cell (design §5): the change request it exercised, whether its delivery materialized/landed, the advance decision and the touch it was seeded from, its outcome/cost, and its exact per-touch scoring blocks (regression, conventions, dimensions, phases).

changeRequestId.description: Stable id of the touch's change request (scenario.json `touches[].id`, e.g. 'credit-notes', 'role-enforcement').

landed.description: Whether the touch's delivery LANDED on the sandbox default branch (mandrel arms: true = merged, false = an unlanded PR-head tree was scored). Null for arms where landing is not a concept (control commits directly) and when undetermined.

materialized.description: Whether a scoreable delivered tree existed for this touch (merged main OR a differing PR-head branch). `false` ⇒ `outcome` is null (unmeasured, never a fabricated 0 on the stale last-good tree) and the touch cannot advance.

advanced.description: The advance decision: delivered && baseSuitePassRate ≥ advanceThreshold && appBoots (and the force-push of the scored tree as the new chain baseline succeeded). `false` ⇒ the next touch seeds from the last-good tree (skip-forward).

seededFromTouch.description: The last-good touch index whose tree this touch's baseline was seeded from; 0 = the pristine scenario seed.

appBoots.description: Whether the delivered tree still boots and reports ready (the third advance gate). Null when unmeasured (unmaterialized delivery).

outcome.description: The touch's composite quality score (`dimensions.quality.score` over the evolved frozen suite: retained base tests + accumulated additions). Null when the delivery did not materialize or the suite produced no verdict.

cost.description: The touch's session cost in USD (its `dimensions.efficiency.costUsd`), or null when the envelope reported none. The BENCH_MAX_COST_USD accounting sums THIS field across the chain.

regression.description: The evolved frozen-suite verdict at this touch (PR-B's suite-evolution runner): retained base tests vs failures/missing, plus the accumulated per-touch additions.

baseTotal.description: Total tests in the frozen base-suite inventory (before supersedes).

retainedTotal.description: Base tests retained at this touch (base − superseded 1..k).

retainedPassed.description: Retained base tests that passed. retainedPassed ÷ retainedTotal is the baseSuitePassRate the advance gate compares against advanceThreshold.

regressionRate.description: Failures (including never-reported tests, conservatively) among retained base tests ÷ retained count.

additionsTotal.description: Frozen behavioural ADDITIONS accumulated across touches 1..k.

conventions.description: Convention grep-oracle verdicts over the delivered tree (PR-B's conventions/, e.g. error-envelope / validation-call / layering / money-integer).

clean.description: True when the delivered tree carries no violation of this convention.

cleanRate.description: Fraction of convention oracles the tree passed clean.

dimensions.description: The FULL dimension set scored for this touch — the same seven-dimension shape as the record's `dimensions`, computed over this touch's delivered tree (exact, unlike the record-level chain aggregate).

phases.description: Per-phase session envelopes for this touch's mandrel-arm plan+deliver run (D-019); absent for control-arm touches (single session).

qualityDimension.description: Quality — is the output correct & on-intent? Spine: the scenario's frozen acceptance suite pass rate; cross-checked by the acceptance-eval LLM judge.

score.description: Composite quality in [0,1]. Formula in README; the frozen-suite pass rate is the objective spine. NULL when the mandrel delivery did not materialize (see the delivery-not-materialized warning) — excluded from the differential rather than scored a false 0.

frozenSuitePassRate.description: Fraction of the scenario's frozen acceptance-suite assertions that passed against the delivered app.

acceptanceEvalScore.description: LLM-judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run (e.g. control arm with no acceptance criteria).

planningFidelityDimension.description: Planning fidelity — did the plan match reality? Decomposition accuracy, re-plan count, plan-vs-actual drift. For the control arm (no plan authored) this is null.

score.description: Planning fidelity in [0,1], or null when no plan was authored (control arm).

rePlanCount.description: Number of re-plan / decomposition-revision events observed.

plannedStoryCount.description: Number of Stories the decomposer emitted.

fileFootprintDrift.description: Symmetric drift between the planned changes[] footprint and the files actually touched (Jaccard distance in [0,1]).

footprintDropped.description: Epic #66, Story #77: true when the declared plan size was known and ≤1 file, in which case the footprint term is dropped from the score's mean (a ≤1-file plan carries too little footprint signal to score reliably — see fileFootprintDrift on a functionally-perfect single-file delivery).

autonomyDimension.description: Autonomy — how little human intervention? Counts of HITL stops, agent::blocked transitions, and manual rescues, collapsed to a [0,1] score where 1 = fully unattended.

score.description: Autonomy in [0,1]; 1 means the run completed with zero human intervention. Null when unmeasured — the mandrel arm produced no lifecycle ledger to read interventions from.

hitlStops.description: Count of human-in-the-loop STOP gates the run halted at.

blockedEvents.description: Count of agent::blocked transitions observed in lifecycle.ndjson.

manualRescues.description: Count of operator interventions that unblocked or restarted the run.

landed.description: Ticket #121, item 2: the unattended-landing datum this autonomy score folds in. `false` (the mandrel PR did not land unattended) counts as one intervention in the autonomy formula — the reliability failure the old formula turned into an invisible null. `true` (landed) or `null` (control / undetermined) adds nothing. Mirrors the top-level `landed` field for the mandrel arm; `null` for the control arm.

guardrail.description: Epic #66, Story #77: autonomy reclassified as a mandrel-arm guardrail (cohort threshold, default 0.99) rather than a mandrel-vs-control delta — a drop below threshold is itself a finding.

threshold.description: Cohort guardrail threshold the score is compared against (default 0.99).

met.description: True when score ≥ threshold, false when below, null when the score itself is unmeasured (an undetermined guardrail is never reported as pass or fail).

maintainabilityDimension.description: Maintainability — how readable and low-complexity is the output? (value side, Story #36). Two-oracle shape: objective spine (static-analysis / linter signal) weighted 0.7 plus LLM judge cross-check weighted 0.3; judge weight folds into spine when cross-check is null.

score.description: Composite maintainability in [0,1]. Objective spine is weighted 0.7; LLM judge cross-check is weighted 0.3. NULL when the mandrel delivery did not materialize (see delivery-not-materialized) — excluded from the differential.

lintWarnings.description: Count of linter warnings emitted against the delivered output.

complexityScore.description: Normalised cyclomatic complexity score in [0,1] where 1 is least complex, or null when not measured.

maintainabilityIndex.description: Normalised maintainability index in [0,1] where 1 is most maintainable, or null when not measured.

maintainabilityJudgeScore.description: LLM judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 loud nulls: operator-visible markers for a missing spine ('maintainability-signal-absent') or a missing judge cross-check ('maintainability-judge-absent'), instead of a silent default/null.

securityDimension.description: Security — how free of vulnerabilities is the output? (value side, Story #36). Two-oracle shape: objective spine (static-analysis / scanner signal) weighted 0.7 plus LLM judge cross-check weighted 0.3; judge weight folds into spine when cross-check is null.

score.description: Composite security in [0,1]. Objective spine is weighted 0.7; LLM judge cross-check is weighted 0.3. Null when the dimension was not validly measured — e.g. cohorts produced before the overlay-confound fix, where the scanner read the overlaid framework tree instead of only the delivered app, have had security cleared to null. NULL when the mandrel delivery did not materialize (see delivery-not-materialized) — excluded from the differential.

criticalFindings.description: Count of critical-severity findings from security scanning.

highFindings.description: Count of high-severity findings from security scanning.

secretsDetected.description: True iff a secret (API key, credential, etc.) was detected in the delivered output.

securityJudgeScore.description: LLM judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 loud nulls: operator-visible markers for missing scan data ('security-signal-absent') or a missing judge cross-check ('security-judge-absent'), instead of a silent default/null.

efficiencyDimension.description: Efficiency — what did it cost absolutely? Wall-clock, tokens, and dispatch count. Cost (tokens) comes from the `claude -p` envelope; timings/dispatches from lifecycle.ndjson. No composite is required — the three are reported as a vector; an optional `costUsd` is included when the envelope reports it.

wallClockMs.description: End-to-end wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds.

totalTokens.description: TRUE, sub-agent-INCLUSIVE total tokens (input + output + cacheRead + cacheWrite), summed from the `claude -p` envelope's `modelUsage` map when present so it matches the sub-agent-inclusive dollar column (Ticket #122, item 1). Before the fix the harness summed only the parent session's top-level `usage`, undercounting a fan-out Epic run 2–3× (14.2M reported vs ~27.9M true) while dollars were already sub-agent-inclusive. When `modelUsage` is absent (or degenerately smaller than the reported figure) this equals `reportedTokens`.

reportedTokens.description: Ticket #122, item 1: the PARENT-session-only token figure — the top-level `usage` envelope total. Recorded alongside the sub-agent-inclusive `totalTokens` so the reported-vs-true gap (the sub-agent overhead the old single figure hid) is legible. Equals `totalTokens` for a control cell (no sub-agents).

inputTokens.description: Fresh input tokens from the (sub-agent-inclusive) token split.

outputTokens.description: Output tokens from the (sub-agent-inclusive) token split. The most expensive kind (~$21.6/M) — kept distinct from cache reads so efficiency scoring never conflates the two (Ticket #122, item 1).

cacheReadTokens.description: Ticket #122, item 1: cache-read tokens (the cheapest kind, ~$0.65/M) from the sub-agent-inclusive split. Persisted separately so a single conflated `totalTokens` no longer equates a cache read with a ~$21.6/M output token.

cacheWriteTokens.description: Ticket #122, item 1: cache-creation (write) tokens from the sub-agent-inclusive split.

dispatches.description: Number of agent dispatches (Story sub-agent launches) recorded in lifecycle.ndjson.

gateRetries.description: Ticket #121, item 2: count of self-recovered close-validate gate failures (`story.blocked` with a `close-validate-failed:*` reason) — moved OFF the autonomy tally (they are self-recovered, already priced in tokens) and reported here as a cost signal instead of dragging every gated run's autonomy to 0.50.

costUsd.description: Total USD cost from the `claude -p` envelope when reported, else null.

overheadRatioDimension.description: Overhead ratio — ceremony tax vs. shippable output. Ceremony cost (planning, decomposition, orchestration, gate ceremony) divided by codegen cost (tokens/time spent producing shippable artifacts). For the control arm there is effectively no ceremony, so tokenRatio approaches its floor.

tokenRatio.description: ceremonyTokens ÷ codegenTokens. A ratio of 4.0 means 4 tokens of ceremony per token of shippable codegen. Null when codegenTokens is 0 (undefined ratio) — e.g. the mandrel arm produced no lifecycle ledger, so no codegen could be attributed.

timeRatio.description: ceremonyMs ÷ codegenMs, or null when the time split is not available.

ceremonyTokens.description: Tokens attributed to ceremony (planning + orchestration + gates).

codegenTokens.description: Tokens attributed to producing shippable code/artifacts.
